title: Recursive orchestration: continuation engine + project checkpoints
description:
Generalize the cross-epic continuation engine to walk the project tree (design §5). Hard-depends on the container model from the data-model epic.

tk next ascends past a finished epic's siblings into the parent container; project boundaries STOP on a checkpoint by default (per-project flag), with a global autonomous-mode override. Epic→epic auto-continue is unchanged; epic retro/learnings are unchanged.

Deliverables:
- recursive frontier ascent in tk next (epic→sibling→parent container)
- project close-out checkpoint default + per-project auto-continue flag
- global autonomous-mode switch (config / run flag)
- regression: project-less roadmap path unchanged (two-tier stopping rule)
notes:
2026-06-20 15:09 - RETRO (epic vdl) — VERDICT SHIP.
Learnings: none promoted — no new high-signal cross-cutting gotchas (the stale-base recurrence is already captured in learnings.md and the prompt's 'merge epic/zt6 first' handled it; the keep-old-signatures + add *WithMode-delegating-false backwards-compat pattern is standard Go practice).
Verification (frontier review): all deliverables present + tested — CompletedProjectsNeedingCloseout, NextPlannableEpics, autonomous-mode config + GetAutonomousMode, gatesHuman checkpoint-only bypass, tk next --autonomous wiring. Backwards-compat: OFF path byte-equivalent, no existing test expectations changed.
Drift: none. Accepted nits (non-blocking, no cleanup tick): redundant awaiting re-filter in next.go; missing nested-project/zero-leaf-project/leaf-checkpoint test cases.
Roadmap changes proposed: none.
closed_reason: Completed: recursive continuation engine merged & green on epic/zt6; final review SHIP; close-out done
